codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning, session 019e5a4e) returned 3 findings with PROPOSED_FIX entries. (1) AC2 only tests baseline_head_sha mismatch — M2 requires both scalars to be equality-verified but AC2 negative case only exercises SHA mismatch; a dev implementing SHA-only blocking would appear to satisfy AC2. (2) QA has no under-report check for untracked created files — D1 closes diff-file omissions but a dev can create an untracked file, omit it from files_created, and pass because it is absent from git diff. (3) agents/changelog-analyst.md excluded despite sharing the same D3 gap — spec logs it out-of-scope but residual risk is present.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (AC2 missing dirty-snapshot negative case): BLOCKING — AC2's check.expect states baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch must block, but the THEN clause only specifies baseline_head_sha mismatch as the negative case; a compliant dev could implement SHA-only blocking and satisfy all literals. Raised as blocking issue. Finding 2 (no under-report check for untracked files): OUT_OF_SCOPE — this is a net-new requirement not contained in the user's original complaint or the three named bugs D1/D2/D3. The user's D1 complaint is specifically about files in git diff not in files_modified; untracked files by definition do not appear in git diff. Rejecting as scope expansion. Finding 3 (changelog-analyst): OBSERVATION_ONLY already accounted for in out_of_scope_observations; spec decision is explicitly documented and correct.

D1_under_reporting_gap.evidence: agents/qa.md:411-422 iterates dev.files_modified and checks each path against git diff output. Steps 1-5 only check the over-reporting direction (is every path in files_modified also in the diff?). No inverse iteration exists. No code iterates git diff --name-only output and checks each diff path against dev.files_modified. The reverse check is structurally absent.
D1_under_reporting_gap.line_evidence: agents/qa.md lines 411-422 — for every path in dev.files_modified → check against diff. No for every path in diff → check against dev.files_modified.

D2_aggregate_missing_provenance_fields.evidence: commands/dev.md:726-762 Aggregate construction rule lists union fields at lines 732-740: dev.status, dev.tasks_completed, dev.scripts_created, dev.permissions_to_add, dev.files_modified, dev.files_created, blocking_issues, recommendations. Neither baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, nor dev.observed_preexisting appear in this list. Example aggregate JSON at lines 745-762 also omits all three fields. Summary at line 702-705 also omits all three.
D2_aggregate_missing_provenance_fields.line_evidence: commands/dev.md:726-762 — full union rule text and example JSON read and confirmed missing fields.

D3_files_created_misses_staged.evidence: agents/dev.md:495 reads: 'dev.files_created: paths from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked new files at end of dev execution)'. The --others flag enumerates only untracked files. A staged file (git add) has an index entry and does NOT appear in --others output. agents/qa.md:427 mirrors the same incomplete command: 'Compute the set of new untracked files: git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'.
D3_files_created_misses_staged.line_evidence: agents/dev.md:495 — git ls-files --others only. agents/qa.md:427 — same.

ac6_verification.finding: AC6 correctly covers the files_created exclusion boundary. Scenario: diff returns staged-new.py, dev.files_modified=[], dev.files_created=['staged-new.py'], baseline_dirty_snapshot=''. THEN clause asserts QA does NOT raise files_modified_underreport_violation because staged-new.py satisfies the union check via dev.files_created. This is the correct boundary test: without the union form (if QA checked only files_modified), this would be a false-positive violation. AC6 is additive and purely tests the union boundary — it does not modify, narrow, or overlap with AC1-AC5.
ac6_verification.ac6_json_check_pattern: The grep pattern in acceptance-criteria JSON covers both orderings: 'files_modified.*union.*files_created|files_created.*union.*files_modified|files_modified UNION files_created|files_created UNION files_modified'. This is adequate.

ac1_through_ac5_preservation.finding: AC1 through AC5 text is identical to iter-1 content modulo the normalization of 'absent from dev.files_modified' to 'absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created' in AC5 AND clause (the iter-2 codex fix that was applied). This normalization is a correction, not a scope narrowing. AC6 is purely additive.

issues.location: docs/dev/ticket-20260524-125300-D.md AC2 THEN clause; docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json AC2 check.expect
issues.finding: AC2's negative case only tests baseline_head_sha mismatch. M2 explicitly requires BOTH top-level baseline_head_sha AND baseline_dirty_snapshot to be equality-verified across workers, with the aggregate blocked if any worker disagrees. However the AC2 THEN clause only states: 'if worker-B instead has baseline_head_sha="xyz999", the aggregate's dev.status is "blocked"'. A dev implementation that blocks only on SHA mismatch and ignores dirty-snapshot mismatch satisfies all AC2 literals while violating M2. The check.expect field in the JSON also does not mention dirty-snapshot mismatch as a blocking case.
issues.recommendation: Add a second negative case to AC2 THEN: 'AND if worker-B has matching baseline_head_sha but different baseline_dirty_snapshot (e.g. baseline_dirty_snapshot: " M extra-file.md" vs orchestrator dispatch value ""), the aggregate dev.status is also "blocked" with a blocking_issue citing baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch.' Update the AC JSON check.expect to include this second blocking scenario.
issues.codex_proposed_fix: Extend AC2 and acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json with a second negative case: if worker-B has matching baseline_head_sha but different baseline_dirty_snapshot, aggregate dev.status must be 'blocked' with a blocking_issue citing baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: QA has no under-report check for untracked created files. D1 closes the diff-file omission direction (files in git diff absent from dev.files_modified). But a dev who creates an untracked file and omits it from dev.files_created entirely will pass: the untracked file is absent from git diff --name-only (untracked files do not appear in diffs), and the existing files_created check iterates dev.files_created, not the actual untracked file set. This is a real adjacent gap but is outside the user's D1/D2/D3 complaint scope — the user specifically described D1 as 'files in the git diff that are NOT in files_modified', not files in untracked set not in files_created.
out_of_scope_observations.codex_proposed_fix: Add requirement/AC for QA to compute actual_created_files = git ls-files --others --exclude-standard UNION git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A, subtract baseline_dirty_paths, then raise files_created_underreport_violation for any remaining path absent from dev.files_created.
out_of_scope_observations.disposition: out_of_scope — separate cycle if user wants to extend D3 to under-report direction

out_of_scope_observations.observation: agents/changelog-analyst.md:127 mirrors the same incomplete git ls-files --others check for files_created provenance anomaly detection (same gap as D3). Spec explicitly logs this in out_of_scope_observations; decision is documented and correct because user-stated fix scope is agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md only.

qa.approval_notes: REJECTED at iteration 2. One blocking issue found: AC2 tests only baseline_head_sha mismatch in its negative case but M2 requires BOTH baseline_head_sha AND baseline_dirty_snapshot to trigger aggregate blocking on mismatch. A dev implementation that blocks SHA mismatch only would satisfy all AC2 literals while violating M2. BA must add a second negative case to AC2 (and the AC JSON check.expect) covering baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch. All three bugs (D1/D2/D3) are confirmed real with direct code evidence. AC6 is correctly scoped and tests the right boundary. All normative text uses the union form. AC1-AC5 are preserved. Two codex findings are out of scope: (1) the files_created under-report check is net-new scope beyond D1-D3; (2) changelog-analyst exclusion is already documented. Fix only BLOCK-1 and resubmit as iteration 3.
